events.title: Lemonade Day
events.prompt: Your neighbor asks if you want to help run a lemonade stand this weekend. You could earn some coins, but you need to spend a little on supplies first.

choices.label: Spend 5 coins on supplies and help out
Say no thanks and save your coins

events.explanation: Spending a little to earn more is called investing. Sometimes you need to spend money to make money!

events.title: Piggy Bank Surprise
events.prompt: You shake your piggy bank and it feels really heavy! You have enough saved to buy a toy you've wanted or keep saving for something bigger.

choices.label: Keep saving for the big toy

events.explanation: Delayed gratification means waiting for a bigger reward instead of taking a smaller one now. It's a superpower for saving!

events.title: Lucky Coin
events.prompt: You found a shiny coin on the ground! What will you do with it?

choices.label: Put it in your savings jar
Spend it at the candy shop

events.explanation: Every small amount adds up over time. Saving even a little bit regularly is how big savings grow!

events.title: Chore Chart Challenge
events.prompt: Your family put up a chore chart. Each chore earns coins. You can do easy chores or try the harder ones that pay more.

choices.label: Do two easy chores (quick and safe)
Try one hard chore (more reward, more effort)

events.explanation: Harder work often pays more, but it takes more effort. Choosing what's right for you is part of earning wisely.

events.title: Sharing Jar
events.prompt: Your class is collecting coins to buy books for kids who don't have any. Do you want to donate some of your coins?

choices.label: Donate 5 coins to the book fund

events.explanation: Giving to others is called generosity. Many people split their money into Save, Spend, and Share jars!

events.title: Sticker vs. Snack
events.prompt: You're at the school store. You see a cool sticker sheet AND your favorite healthy snack, but you only have enough coins for one.
events.explanation: A need is something you must have (like food). A want is something nice to have. Learning the difference helps you spend wisely!

events.title: Birthday Bonanza
events.prompt: Happy Birthday! Grandma gave you 20 coins. What's your plan?

choices.label: Spend it all at the toy shop

events.explanation: When you get a lump sum, splitting it into save/spend/share buckets helps you enjoy it AND build your savings.

events.title: Oops! Broken Toy
events.prompt: Oh no — your favorite toy broke! You can try to fix it for free or buy a new one.
events.explanation: Before buying something new, see if you can fix what you have. Being resourceful saves money!
